items.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Post Weekly Losses as Tech Sell-Off Continues
items.content: S&P 500 and Nasdaq fell for third straight day on June 26. Dow set to close near record high of 52,100. Tech sell-off deepened with chip stocks leading declines.

items.title: Nvidia, Alphabet sit out megacap tech bounce as chip stocks sink
items.content: Chip stocks sold off on June 26 amid concerns over rising AI infrastructure costs. Intel -3%, Sandisk -10%, Arm -4%, Marvell -5%, Micron -5%. SMIC tumbled nearly 7%. Global chip rout extended to Europe (ASML -2%, Infineon -4%).

items.title: Hang Seng Falls 1.8% to 22,672 as AI Chip Rout and IPO Market Uncertainty
items.content: Hang Seng Index closed June 26 at 22,671-22,672, a one-year low, down 1.8% on AI chip sell-off. Hong Kong's stock market hit hardest by global tech rout.

items.title: May PCE inflation tops 4%, leaves Fed hike on the table
items.content: Core PCE hit 3.4% annual rate (highest since Oct 2023). Headline PCE at 4.1% (highest since April 2023). Energy prices tied to Iran war drove gains. Markets expect Fed rate hike in September. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh faces pressure.

items.title: May US PCE inflation tops 4%, leaves Fed hike on the table
items.content: US PCE inflation measure topped 4% in May, consumer spending strong. Fed's preferred gauge shows inflation accelerating, keeping rate hike on table for September.

items.title: US PCE Price Index Expected 0.5% MoM in May
items.content: US PCE price index expected to rise 0.5% month-over-month in May 2026, accelerating from 0.4% in April, as higher energy costs linked to Iran conflict continue to feed through.

items.title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis - renewed ceasefire tensions
items.content: On June 19, Trump announced renewed ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. On June 20, Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz citing Israeli violations. On June 27, US Navy announced widened route through Strait of Hormuz. Guardian reports fresh hostilities.

items.title: Fresh hostilities in Gulf suggest US-Iran memorandum was too broadly worded
items.content: Renewed tensions in the Gulf as ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah fails to hold. Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz. Memorandum of understanding between US and Iran faces implementation challenges.

items.title: China's technology IPO market rebounds on AI and semiconductor push
items.content: Technology companies raised combined $3.1 billion through stock market listings in China between Jan-Jun 18, driven by AI and semiconductor push amid US rivalry.

items.title: SSE Composite Index Holds Near 4,120 as AI Stocks Rally and Micron Earnings Lift China Tech
items.content: Shanghai Composite closed at approx 4,120 on June 25 (+0.23%). AI stocks rallied, Micron earnings lifted China tech sentiment despite global sell-off.

items.title: Micron tops Q3 earnings estimates, offers better-than-expected outlook
items.content: Micron (MU) reported fiscal Q3 results above estimates, benefiting from AI-driven memory demand. However, stock fell >5% on Friday as broader chip rout continued.
